content:
# Revelation Chapter 3 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus, pages 279-280 | **Source Image:** REV_003_page_001.jpg, REV_003_page_002.jpg

1. And to the angel of the assembly in Sardeis write: These [things] says the [one] having the seven spirits of the God and the seven stars: I know of you the works, that a name you have that you live, and dead you are.

2. Become watching, and strengthen the remaining [things] which were about to die, for I have not found the works of you having been fulfilled before the God of me.

3. Remember therefore how you have received and heard, and keep [it] and repent. If therefore you do not watch, I will come as a thief, and you will not know what hour I will come upon you.

4. But you have a few names in Sardeis which did not defile the garments of them, and they will walk with me in white, because worthy they are.

5. The [one] overcoming, thus will be clothed in white garments, and I will not blot out the name of him from the scroll of the life, and I will confess the name of him before the Father of me and before the angels of him.

6. The [one] having an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies.

7. And to the angel of the assembly in Philadelpheia write: These [things] says the Holy [One], the True [One], the [one] having the key of the David, the [one] opening and no one will shut, and shutting and no one opens:

8. I know of you the works — behold, I have given before you a door having been opened, which no one is able to shut it — that a little power you have, and you kept the word of me and did not deny the name of me.

9. Behold, I give from the synagogue of the Satanas, the [ones] saying themselves to be Ioudaioi and are not, but lie — behold, I will make them that they will come and will bow down before the feet of you, and they will know that I loved you.

10. Because you kept the word of the endurance of me, I also will keep you from the hour of the testing, the [one] being about to come upon the whole inhabited [earth], to test the [ones] dwelling upon the earth.

11. I am coming swiftly. Hold what you have, that no one take the crown of you.

12. The [one] overcoming, I will make him a pillar in the temple of the God of me, and outside he will not go out any more, and I will write upon him the name of the God of me and the name of the city of the God of me, the new Ierousalem, the [one] descending out of the heaven from the God of me, and the name of me, the new.

13. The [one] having an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies.

14. And to the angel of the assembly in Laodikeia write: These [things] says the Amen, the witness, the faithful and true, the beginning of the creation of the God:

15. I know of you the works, that neither cold you are nor hot. Would that cold you were or hot.

16. So, because lukewarm you are, and neither hot nor cold, I am about to vomit you out of the mouth of me.

17. Because you say that rich I am and I have become wealthy and of nothing I have need, and you do not know that you are the wretched and pitiable and poor and blind and naked,

18. I counsel you to buy from me gold having been fired from fire, that you may become rich, and white garments, that you may clothe [yourself] and the shame of the nakedness of you may not be made manifest, and eye-salve to anoint the eyes of you, that you may see.

19. I, as many as I love, I reprove and discipline. Be zealous therefore and repent.

20. Behold, I have stood at the door and I knock. If anyone hears the voice of me and opens the door, I will come in to him and will dine with him and he with me.

21. The [one] overcoming, I will give to him to sit with me on the throne of me, as also I overcame and sat with the Father of me on the throne of him.

22. The [one] having an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **3:1 — "dead you are":** Sinaiticus preserves the predicate-first construction ΝΕΚΡΟΣ ΕΙ, which EMET renders literally. The abruptness of "dead you are" following "you have a name that you live" creates the sharp ironic contrast intended by the text.

2. **3:2 — "having been fulfilled":** Sinaiticus reads ΠΕΠΛΗΡΩΜΕΝΑ (perfect passive participle), indicating the works were found in a state of incompleteness. Some manuscripts read differently.

3. **3:4 — "names":** The use of ΟΝΟΜΑΤΑ ("names") to mean "persons" is a Semitism. EMET preserves the literal Greek rather than translating as "people."

4. **3:7 — "the key of the David":** Sinaiticus includes the article with David (ΤΟΥ ΔΑΥΕΙΔ), alluding to Isaiah 22:22. The singular "key" (not "keys" as in 1:18) is significant.

5. **3:8 — Anacoluthon:** The syntax of v.8 is broken — a parenthetical statement about the open door interrupts the main clause. EMET preserves this grammatical disruption with dashes rather than smoothing the syntax.

6. **3:9 — "I give from":** The Greek ΔΙΔΩ ΕΚ is an unusual construction. EMET preserves the preposition literally. The sense is "I give [some] from the synagogue of Satan" — i.e., I will deliver them to you.

7. **3:12 — Triple naming:** The overcomer receives three names inscribed: (1) the name of God, (2) the name of the new Jerusalem, (3) the new name of Christ. This triple inscription echoes the high priest's forehead plate.

8. **3:14 — "the Amen":** Christ is called Ο ΑΜΗΝ — the Amen personified. This unique title in the NT draws from Isaiah 65:16 LXX where God is called "the God of the Amen" (the God of truth).

9. **3:16 — "vomit" (εμεσαι):** Sinaiticus reads ΕΜΕΣΑΙ, the aorist infinitive of ΕΜΕΩ. EMET preserves this visceral image. The hot mineral springs near Laodicea, which became lukewarm and emetic by the time they reached the city, provide the local background.

10. **3:17 — Five adjectives:** The Greek piles five predicate adjectives without conjunctions (asyndeton): wretched, pitiable, poor, blind, naked. This rapid accumulation intensifies the rebuke.

11. **3:20 — "I have stood":** Sinaiticus reads ΕΣΤΗΚΑ (perfect tense) — "I have stood [and remain standing]," emphasizing the ongoing, patient posture of Christ at the door. EMET preserves the perfect tense.

— EMET
